metadata.title:
SPOKANE DRAWS
CONGRESSMAN

DISTANT PARTS OF COUNTRY
ARE ALREADY CONCERNED
IN THE NATIONAL lRRlGA-
TON CONGRESS NEXT SUM.
MER.

pages.raw_text:
Congressman Richmond Pearson
Hobson of Alabama writes in let
ter from Washington, D. c., to R,
Insinger, chairman of the local
board of control of the seventeenth
national irrigation congress to meet
in Spokane, August 8 to IA, that
irrigation, forestry, deep water
ways and BODYBUILDING are of vital
interest to the people of his state.


United States Senator John II.
Bankhead of Alabama writes that
he will attend the convention, add
ing: Irrigation is doing a great
work for the country and l hope
for it every possible success"


Congressman o. w. Underwood
of Alabama, who was a member
Of the congressional committee
which originated and fathered the
bill providing for the irrigation of
the arid west, writes that he is
deeply interested in the subject,
but that the people of the state
have vital interest in forestry,
drainage, good roads, deep water
ways and BODYBUILDING


Congressman II. l.. Goodwin of
North Carolina says that the PEG.
pie of his state are much inter
ested in the subjects of drainage,
forestry, deep waterways and
BODYBUILDING.


Congressman John d. Small of
North Carolina writes that the
eastern part of his state is inter.
ested in drainage, forestry and
deep waterways, and that he will
attend the convention in Spokane
II possible.


Mr.. Insinger has also received
letters from other congressional
representatives in the south, mani
Testing interest in the Spokane
congress.
